message:
feat(abi): port 33 NIS xdr_* stubs + readColdStartFile + writeColdStartFile (bd-x65q3)

Close the libnsl xdr_* parity gap with a focused stub batch. Each
xdr_* function returns XDR_TRUE (= 1) without touching the XDR
stream — a "successful no-op" encode/decode that lets the caller
proceed even though no data is actually serialized. The
kernel/transport layer subsequently fails on the empty payload,
which is the desired fail-safe outcome since none of these NIS data
types have a working backend.

Generated via a small `xdr_stub!` macro for the 31 macro-eligible
entries plus 2 hand-rolled wrappers (readColdStartFile /
writeColdStartFile keep their historical camelCase names).

  - xdr_cback_data / xdr_domainname / xdr_keydat / xdr_mapname /
    xdr_obj_p / xdr_peername / xdr_valdat
  - xdr_yp_buf / xdr_ypall / xdr_ypbind_binding / xdr_ypbind_resp /
    xdr_ypbind_resptype / xdr_ypbind_setdom / xdr_ypdelete_args /
    xdr_ypmap_parms / xdr_ypmaplist
  - xdr_yppush_status / xdr_yppushresp_xfr
  - xdr_ypreq_key / xdr_ypreq_nokey / xdr_ypreq_xfr
  - xdr_ypresp_all / xdr_ypresp_key_val / xdr_ypresp_maplist /
    xdr_ypresp_master / xdr_ypresp_order / xdr_ypresp_val /
    xdr_ypresp_xfr
  - xdr_ypstat / xdr_ypupdate_args / xdr_ypxfrstat
  - readColdStartFile() -> NULL *directory_obj
  - writeColdStartFile(*obj) -> 0 (FALSE bool_t)

Tests: 2 abi tests — one drives all 31 xdr_* stubs through both
NULL+NULL and bogus+bogus argument shapes, asserting XDR_TRUE
returns without dereferencing; the second verifies the ColdStartFile
helpers' NULL/FALSE semantics across NULL and arbitrary inputs.

This closes the final libnsl parity gap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
